samples.visual: Lean English officer-type man, mid-30s to 40s, period moustache, shirt-sleeves under a waterproof coat; angular face, weathered outdoor skin, short neat hair.
Adult lean gray Indian wolf, male, rangy muscle under coarse gray coat, pale underbelly, amber eyes, long muzzle, alert ears.
Great gray she-wolf (Raksha), heavy maternal build, big gray nose, thick ruff, green-reflecting eyes, powerful shoulders.
Four small gray wolf cubs, soft puppy coats, oversized paws and ears, dark muzzle masks, bright curious eyes.
Indian jackal, smaller than the wolves, dusty scruffy coat, bushy tail, pointed muzzle, bright eyes, low posture, lean ribs.
Huge adult male Bengal tiger, great square head and massive shoulders, rich orange coat with bold black stripes, white ruffs, amber eyes; permanently lame in on
Indian human male: brown skin, long dark hair, hard bare feet; ages from naked soft dimpled baby just walking, to bare jungle child about 7, to lean athletic ne
Great gray Lone Wolf, pack leader: large mature male, full gray coat, heavier bone than Father Wolf, noble muzzle; ages to thinner, stiffer old wolf.
Young adult Seeonee wolf in fourth year, leaner and darker-mixed gray than Akela, intense eyes.
Large old brown sloth bear, heavy body, shaggy brown coat, blunt muzzle, small eyes, big padded paws, furry stomach.
Adult black panther (melanistic leopard), inky black coat with ghost rosettes visible as watered-silk sheen in light, sleek muscular body, soft fur, green eyes,
Troop of gray langur-like monkeys—long limbs, gray fur, black faces and hands, restless tails.
Large kite (bird of prey), brown and white underwings, hooked beak, very keen eyes, long wings.
Huge rock python about thirty feet, thick muscular coils, gleaming patterned scales, scarred nose, wedge head, bright eyes.
Indian cobras—hooded, scaled bodies; dusty ruin-dwelling snakes smaller than Kaa.
Young gray wolf, one of Mother Wolf’s litter grown—lean Seeonee gray coat, soft gray nose, pack-brother build.
Gaunt adult pack wolf, mangy tattered coat, shot-scarred or trap-lame look, hollow flanks.
Kind-faced Indian village woman, adult, warm brown skin, dark hair, soft maternal eyes; simple rural sari/cloth of a Waingunga-plain household.
Big fat Indian village priest, white clothes, red-and-yellow caste mark on forehead, heavy belly and jowls.
Adult Indian village hunter, lean weathered man with Tower musket, waist-cloth.
Crowd of North Indian plain villagers—men, women, children in dhotis, saris, turbans; herd-boys, yellow pariah dogs at edges.
Huge fifteen-year-old gray fur seal bull, nearly a mane on shoulders, long wicked dog-teeth, scarred all over from fights; stands over four feet on flippers, ab
Adult female fur seal, gentler softer bulk than Sea Catch, smooth dark coat, maternal eyes.
The White Seal: born with pale watery-blue eyes, large head and shoulders; grows to pure white fur, full-grown sea-catch bulk with curly white mane, heavy build
Young adult dark fur seal about Kotick’s age, beach-fighter bulk, dark coat without white.
Mass of fur seals—bulls, cows, holluschickie bachelors, pups—dark coats in dense groups on basalt and sand.
Great sea-cow (manatee-like), huge slow gray-brown body, calm face.
Small Indian mongoose, catlike fur, weasel-like head, pink eyes and restless pink nose, bottle-brush tail when roused, ivory fangs.
Small English boy in Segowlee cantonment, fair colonial child, period light clothes, big eyes.
Teddy’s Mother—English woman Alice, adult, kind face, period bungalow dress.
Big Englishman, adult male, period colonial shirt and trousers, solid paternal build; moustache optional.
Tailor-bird male, small songbird, olive-brown upperparts, pale underparts, long tail feathers.
Female tailor-bird, slightly duller small songbird, same species as Darzee.
Big black cobra, five feet long, spectacled hood spread, dull glowing eyes, thick body; spectacle mark on hood.
Adult female cobra, sleek hooded body, black-olive sheen matching Nag, intense eyes.
Muskrat, small brown wet-furred rodent, timid low posture along walls, whiskered pointed face.
Great Asian elephant nearly seventy years old; ten feet at the shoulder, dark wrinkled hide, tusks cut short at five feet and bound with copper bands; large eye
Weathered adult Indian mahout, dark sun-leathered skin, strong hands, working camp clothes; adult father build.
Indian boy ten years old, four feet high, sun-bleached brown hair flying loose, barefoot jungle-camp child in a single rag garment.
English Keddah chief, adult man, field mustache, solar-topee era colonial hunting dress.
Head tracker, adult Assamese/jungle Indian man, lean scarred hunter build, gun and field cloth.
Clever adult she-elephant, slightly smaller than Kala Nag, working government elephant, calm intelligent eye.
Young Indian woman in simple sari, soft maternal face.
Sharp fox terrier, white with brown markings, small dog carriage.
Adult camp man, period soldier-servant clothes, wet camp kit.
Tough screw-gun mule, straps rings and chains on saddle-pad, campaigner build; breech-piece mule of number two gun, First Screw Battery.
Large baggage camel of the 39th Native Infantry, soft feet, long bobbing neck, humped bulk.
Big Australian cavalry mount, Number Fifteen, E troop, Ninth Lancers; parade-fit, silky tail when groomed, one ear forward one back.
Pair of enormous patient gun-bullocks, heavy flanks, heavy yokes, slaty working cattle build.
Huge gun-team elephant, forty years’ service, harnessed to siege gun; trunk and dual-end bulk.
Amir of Afghanistan, Central Asian king, high big black astrakhan-wool hat, great diamond star, horseman in royal riding dress.

results.promptLabel: Enhanced rules for animal species vs human vs collective groups
results.note: Adds explicit guidance for individual talking animals vs collective packs/crowds
